proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs between patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab and those not receiving it.
Mean pfs_months differs between patients receiving treatment_sotorasib and those not receiving it.
Mean pfs_months differs between patients receiving treatment_olaparib and those not receiving it.
Mean pfs_months differs between patients receiving treatment_osimertinib and those not receiving it.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS = 3.414 mo on pembrolizumab vs 3.449 mo off (diff = -0.035, t-test p = 0.081). No overall benefit of pembrolizumab on PFS.
Mean PFS = 3.641 mo on sotorasib vs 3.319 mo off (diff = +0.322, t-test p = 1.6e-53). Sotorasib confers a small but highly significant overall PFS gain.
Mean PFS = 3.414 mo on olaparib vs 3.439 mo off (diff = -0.025, t-test p = 0.255). No overall PFS benefit.
Mean PFS = 3.434 mo on osimertinib vs 3.431 mo off (diff = +0.004, p = 0.870). No overall PFS benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with kras_g12c=1 have higher mean pfs_months than patients with kras_g12c=0.
Patients with egfr_mutation=1 have higher mean pfs_months than patients with egfr_mutation=0.
Patients with alk_fusion=1 have lower mean pfs_months than patients with alk_fusion=0.
Patients with tmb_high=1 have higher mean pfs_months than patients with tmb_high=0.
Higher pdl1_tps is associated with higher pfs_months.
Patients with brca2_mutation=1 have higher mean pfs_months than patients with brca2_mutation=0.
Patients with stk11_mutation=1 have lower mean pfs_months than patients with stk11_mutation=0.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS 4.114 (G12C+) vs 3.332 (G12C-), diff +0.782, p=4.8e-151. KRAS G12C+ patients have substantially higher PFS, but this is largely mediated by sotorasib (see iteration 3).
Mean PFS 3.596 (EGFR+) vs 3.407 (EGFR-), diff +0.189, p=1.9e-10.
Mean PFS 2.661 (ALK+) vs 3.472 (ALK-), diff -0.810, p=2.9e-69. ALK fusion is a poor prognostic marker (and may suppress sotorasib benefit – see later).
Mean PFS 3.296 (TMB-high) vs 3.486 (TMB-low), diff -0.190, p=9.1e-18. Direction OPPOSITE to hypothesis: TMB-high patients have shorter PFS.
Coefficient on pdl1_tps = +0.067 mo per unit of TPS (range 0–0.8), p=0.157. PD-L1 is not significantly associated with PFS overall.
Mean PFS 3.316 (BRCA2+) vs 3.435 (BRCA2-), diff -0.119, p=0.041. Direction OPPOSITE to hypothesis: BRCA2 mutation associated with slightly worse PFS.
Mean PFS 3.439 (STK11+) vs 3.430 (STK11-), diff +0.009, p=0.746. STK11 mutation has no detectable effect on PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of treatment_sotorasib on pfs_months is larger in patients with kras_g12c=1 than in those with kras_g12c=0 (positive interaction).
The effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months increases with pdl1_tps (positive interaction).
The effect of treatment_olaparib on pfs_months is larger in patients with brca2_mutation=1 than in those with brca2_mutation=0 (positive interaction).
The effect of treatment_osimertinib on pfs_months is larger in patients with egfr_mutation=1 than in those with egfr_mutation=0 (positive interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = +2.558 mo (p ≈ 0). Sotorasib has essentially no effect in KRAS G12C- patients (-0.005 mo) and a +2.553 mo gain in G12C+ patients. Strongly supports a sotorasib×G12C synergy.
Interaction coefficient = -0.116 mo per unit pdl1_tps, p = 0.217. No PD-L1-driven enhancement of pembrolizumab efficacy; if anything, the point estimate is in the opposite direction.
Interaction coefficient = +0.037 mo, p = 0.778. No detectable BRCA2-driven enhancement of olaparib efficacy.
Interaction coefficient = -0.020 mo, p = 0.758. No detectable EGFR-driven enhancement of osimertinib efficacy. Surprising given the targeted-therapy biology.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is larger in patients with tmb_high=1 than in those with tmb_high=0.
Within KRAS G12C+ patients, treatment_sotorasib increases pfs_months substantially.
Within KRAS G12C- patients, treatment_sotorasib has no effect on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = -0.018, p = 0.685. TMB-high status does not modify pembrolizumab efficacy.
Within KRAS G12C+ (n=6368): mean PFS 5.766 on sotorasib vs 3.213 off, diff = +2.552, p = 2.5e-275.
Within KRAS G12C- (n=43632): mean PFS 3.329 on vs 3.334 off, diff = -0.005, p = 0.799. Confirms no benefit of sotorasib outside G12C+.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Sex modifies the sotorasib effect within KRAS G12C+ patients: the benefit is concentrated in males (sex_female=0).
Within KRAS G12C+ male (sex_female=0) patients, treatment_sotorasib raises pfs_months by >4 months on average.
Within KRAS G12C+ female (sex_female=1) patients, treatment_sotorasib has no effect on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: 3-way interaction coefficient = -4.688, p ≈ 0. The treatment_sotorasib:kras_g12c effect of +4.663 mo is essentially eliminated by sex_female (3-way coefficient nearly equal and opposite).
G12C+ males (n=3508): sotorasib on mean 7.857 vs off 3.213; diff = +4.644 mo, p ≈ 0.
G12C+ females (n=2860): sotorasib on mean 3.211 vs off 3.223; diff = -0.012 mo, p = 0.884. Zero benefit in females.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within KRAS G12C+ males, alk_fusion=1 (rare overlap) eliminates the benefit of sotorasib on pfs_months.
Within KRAS G12C+ males, brca2_mutation=1 eliminates the benefit of sotorasib on pfs_months.
Within KRAS G12C+ males without alk_fusion and without brca2_mutation, sotorasib confers a PFS benefit of approximately +5 months.

analyses.result_summary: G12C+ male alk_fusion+ (n=138): sotorasib diff = -0.285 mo, p = 0.511. ALK fusion fully suppresses the sotorasib benefit.
G12C+ male brca2_mutation+ (n=107): sotorasib diff = -0.068 mo, p = 0.883. BRCA2 mutation fully suppresses the sotorasib benefit.
G12C+ male, alk-, brca2- (n=3266): sotorasib on mean 8.234 (n=1154) vs off 3.249 (n=2112); diff = +4.985 mo, p ≈ 0. Largest treatment effect anywhere in the dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: ALK fusion attenuates the sotorasib×kras_g12c interaction effect on pfs_months in a multivariable interaction model.
BRCA2 mutation attenuates the sotorasib×kras_g12c interaction effect on pfs_months in a multivariable interaction model.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_sotorasib * kras_g12c * sex_female + treatment_sotorasib:alk_fusion + treatment_sotorasib:brca2_mutation', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Joint model: treatment_sotorasib:kras_g12c = +4.650 (p ≈ 0); treatment_sotorasib:kras_g12c:sex_female = -4.684 (p ≈ 0); treatment_sotorasib:alk_fusion = -1.049 (p = 2.2e-45); treatment_sotorasib:brca2_mutation = -0.349 (p = 1.8e-4). All four modifier effects are statistically significant; ALK fusion and BRCA2 mutation independently subtract from the sotorasib treatment effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: ECOG performance status (ecog_ps) is inversely associated with pfs_months.
stage_iv=1 patients have lower pfs_months than stage_iv=0 patients.
has_brain_mets=1 patients have lower pfs_months than has_brain_mets=0 patients.
Patients with squamous histology have lower pfs_months than adenocarcinoma patients.
Female patients (sex_female=1) have lower pfs_months than male patients.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS by ECOG: 4.327 (ECOG 0), 3.185 (1), 2.152 (2). Linear coefficient = -1.10 mo per unit ECOG, p ≈ 0.
Mean PFS 2.925 (stage IV) vs 4.362 (non-stage IV), diff = -1.438 mo, p ≈ 0.
Mean PFS 2.743 (mets+) vs 3.663 (mets-), diff = -0.921 mo, p ≈ 0.
Mean PFS 2.813 (squamous) vs 3.670 (adeno), diff = -0.857 mo, p ≈ 0.
Mean PFS 3.319 (female) vs 3.523 (male), diff = -0.204 mo, p = 2.4e-24.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher pfs_months.
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower pfs_months.
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with lower pfs_months.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower pfs_months.
Higher nlr is associated with lower pfs_months.
Older age (age_years) is associated with lower pfs_months (higher age, worse outcomes is the standard direction).

analyses.result_summary: Coefficient = +0.441 mo per g/dL, p = 2.8e-109. Higher albumin → higher PFS.
Coefficient = -0.073 mo per percent, p = 7.5e-179. Higher weight loss → lower PFS.
Coefficient = -5.0e-4 mo per U/L, p = 3.6e-5. Higher LDH → lower PFS.
Coefficient = +3.0e-4 mo per mg/L, p = 0.776. CRP not associated with PFS univariately.
Coefficient = +0.004 mo per unit NLR, p = 0.416. NLR not associated with PFS univariately.
Coefficient = +0.170 mo per year, p ≈ 0. Direction OPPOSITE to the standard hypothesis: older age → HIGHER PFS in this synthetic cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Current smokers have lower pfs_months than former or never smokers.
After multivariable adjustment for clinical/laboratory features, pdl1_tps remains independently associated with pfs_months.
After multivariable adjustment, kras_g12c remains independently associated with pfs_months (positive direction) due to sotorasib treatment effects in G12C+ males.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS: current 2.987, former 3.607, never 3.675. Joint OLS shows smoking_status[former]=+0.55 (p≈0) and [never]=+0.55 (p≈0) versus current.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ pdl1_tps + age_years + ecog_ps + stage_iv + has_brain_mets + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo + ldh_u_l + sex_female + C(histology) + C(smoking_status) + egfr_mutation + kras_g12c + alk_fusion + stk11_mutation + brca2_mutation + tmb_high', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient on pdl1_tps = +0.013, p = 0.426. PD-L1 is NOT independently associated with PFS after adjustment.

Adjusted coefficient on kras_g12c = +0.893, p ≈ 0. KRAS G12C remains a major positive predictor of PFS (driven by the sotorasib effect in G12C+ males, which leaks into the marginal effect because sotorasib is randomly assigned).

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive treatment_pembrolizumab × stage_iv interaction on pfs_months (pembrolizumab harm in localized but not stage IV disease).
There is a positive treatment_pembrolizumab × weight_loss_pct_6mo interaction (pembrolizumab is less harmful or more beneficial in cachectic patients).
treatment_pembrolizumab × pdl1_tps × tmb_high three-way interaction is non-zero on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = +0.108, p = 0.0068. Pembro: stage_iv=0 diff -0.102 (p=0.0021); stage_iv=1 diff +0.006 (p=0.81). Pembrolizumab appears slightly harmful in non-stage-IV disease and null in stage IV.

Interaction coefficient = +0.0184 mo per pct of weight loss, p = 3.2e-4. In low-weight-loss patients, pembro confers -0.111 mo (p=0.001); in high-weight-loss tertile, +0.042 mo (NS). Pembro is harmful in healthier patients.
3-way interaction coefficient = +0.352, p = 0.091. Suggestive but not significant – PD-L1 and TMB do not jointly identify a pembro-responsive subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The pembrolizumab × pdl1_tps interaction is positive within adenocarcinoma histology (pembrolizumab benefits high-PDL1 adeno).
Within high-PDL1 (≥0.5) adenocarcinoma patients, treatment_pembrolizumab raises pfs_months.
Within never-smokers with adenocarcinoma, treatment_pembrolizumab × pdl1_tps interaction is positive.

analyses.result_summary: Within adeno (n=36099): interaction coefficient on pdl1_tps = -0.181, p = 0.130. No positive interaction.
High-PDL1 adeno (n=7756): pembro on 3.649 vs off 3.727, diff = -0.079, p = 0.121. No benefit.
n=7075. Interaction coefficient = +0.137, p = 0.561. No benefit detected even in this canonical IO-responsive niche.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_olaparib × crp_mg_l interaction on pfs_months is positive (more benefit in inflammatory states).
treatment_olaparib × bun_mg_dl interaction on pfs_months is positive.
Within BRCA2 mutation positive patients, treatment_olaparib raises pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction = +0.0055, p = 0.022. Suggestive nominal interaction; effect size is tiny and likely a multiple-testing artifact.
Interaction = +0.011, p = 0.014. Stratified by BUN tertile: low diff -0.069, mid -0.057, high +0.063. Pattern is weak and noisy.
BRCA2+ (n=1519): olaparib on 3.302 vs off 3.323, diff = -0.022, p ~ 0.85. NO benefit of olaparib in BRCA2-mutant patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_osimertinib × alk_fusion interaction on pfs_months is non-zero (off-target ALK effect).
Within EGFR mutation positive patients, treatment_osimertinib raises pfs_months.
Across all four treatments, no PFS benefit is detectable in the sex_female=1, kras_g12c=0 stratum.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = +0.238, p = 0.017. In ALK-fusion+ patients (n=2464), osimertinib appears beneficial, though small sample. Possible nominal off-target signal.
EGFR+ (n=6517): osimertinib on 3.578 vs off 3.604, diff = -0.027, p ~ 0.74. NO osimertinib benefit even in EGFR mutants.

analyses.code: stratified t-tests within sex_female==1 & kras_g12c==0 for each treatment
analyses.result_summary: Within this 21,605-patient subgroup: pembro diff -0.024 (p=0.40); sotorasib diff +0.020 (p=0.49); olaparib diff -0.018 (p=0.55); osimertinib diff +0.013 (p=0.66). No treatment helps these patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Sotorasib effect on pfs_months in KRAS G12C+ males is approximately additive in the linear scale (no further interaction with stage_iv, has_brain_mets, ECOG, smoking, or histology).
Within KRAS G12C+ males (alk-, brca2-), sotorasib confers a benefit ≥ 4 months across every ECOG stratum.
Within KRAS G12C+ females, no continuous lab feature (age, albumin, NLR, LDH, CRP, weight loss) recovers a sotorasib benefit (interaction p>0.10 for all).

analyses.result_summary: Sotorasib diff: stk11=0 +2.59 (p~0); stk11=1 +2.33; brain_mets=0 +2.63; brain_mets=1 +2.33; stage_iv=0 +2.61; stage_iv=1 +2.51; tmb_high=0 +2.57; tmb=1 +2.51; smoke=current +4.52; former +4.78; never +3.98; ECOG 0/1/2 +4.57/+4.77/+4.53. Effect is broadly additive within G12C+ males.

analyses.code: OLS interactions within G12C+ female subset
analyses.result_summary: Within G12C+ females (n=2860): all sotorasib×continuous interactions p>0.1 (age 0.34, albumin 0.55, NLR 0.77, LDH 0.41, CRP 0.62, weight loss 0.41). No measurable feature converts G12C+ females into responders.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for age, ECOG, stage_iv, brain_mets, albumin, weight_loss, ldh, sex, histology, smoking, and all biomarkers/treatments, no significant pembrolizumab main effect remains.
After adjusting for the same covariates, no significant olaparib main effect remains.
After adjusting for the same covariates, no significant osimertinib main effect remains.

analyses.code: Single OLS with PFS ~ all covariates + four treatments
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted main effects (with 95% CIs not shown for brevity): treatment_pembrolizumab ≈ -0.04 (NS); treatment_olaparib ≈ -0.02 (NS); treatment_osimertinib ≈ +0.005 (NS). Only treatment_sotorasib (in interaction with KRAS G12C and sex) yields a robust effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment-effect heterogeneity for treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months: a screen of pembrolizumab × X interactions over all clinical/biomarker features yields no signal that survives Bonferroni correction.
Treatment-effect heterogeneity for treatment_olaparib: same screen yields no signal that survives Bonferroni correction.
Treatment-effect heterogeneity for treatment_osimertinib: same screen yields no signal that survives Bonferroni correction.

analyses.code: Loop OLS interaction screen for treatment_pembrolizumab over ~30 candidate moderators
analyses.result_summary: Smallest interaction p-value for pembrolizumab is treatment_pembrolizumab:weight_loss_pct_6mo (p=3.2e-4); after Bonferroni correction across ~30 tests (alpha=0.0017) this is borderline. Stage_iv interaction p=0.007; nothing else <0.05. Effect sizes are small (<0.12 mo). No clinically meaningful pembrolizumab subgroup.

analyses.code: Loop OLS interaction screen for treatment_olaparib
analyses.result_summary: Smallest interaction p-values: bun_mg_dl 0.014, crp_mg_l 0.022, stk11_mutation 0.073. None survive Bonferroni; no plausible mechanistic pattern. No olaparib subgroup identified.

analyses.code: Loop OLS interaction screen for treatment_osimertinib
analyses.result_summary: Smallest interaction p-values: alk_fusion 0.017, brca2_mutation 0.050. Effect sizes small; not robust to multiplicity. No osimertinib subgroup identified.

proposed_hypotheses.text: An exhaustive 2-feature subgroup search (binary feature pairs) for treatment_pembrolizumab does not identify a subgroup with a clinically meaningful (>0.5 mo) and statistically robust PFS gain.
An exhaustive 2-feature subgroup search for treatment_olaparib does not identify a subgroup with a clinically meaningful (>0.5 mo) and statistically robust PFS gain.
An exhaustive 2-feature subgroup search for treatment_osimertinib does not identify a subgroup with a clinically meaningful (>0.5 mo) and statistically robust PFS gain.

analyses.code: for tx in [pembro, olaparib, osimertinib]: for f1,f2 in combinations(binary_features, 2): for v1,v2 in product([0,1],[0,1]): compute t-test of tx in subset
analyses.result_summary: Across ~10×9×4 ≈ 360 candidate subgroups per treatment, the largest within-subgroup pembrolizumab effect was diff = +0.18 mo (kras_g12c=1, sex_female=1, n~2860) with p ≈ 0.10; the largest olaparib effect was diff = +0.13 mo (alk_fusion=1, brca2_mutation=0); the largest osimertinib effect was diff = +0.22 mo (alk_fusion=1, n=2464, p=0.06). None survive multiple-testing correction; none are clinically meaningful relative to the +5-month sotorasib effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Joint multivariable model for pfs_months: ECOG, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, histology, smoking, age, albumin, weight_loss, ldh, sex, and the sotorasib×kras_g12c×sex three-way interaction together capture the systematic structure; other lab features (sodium, potassium, calcium, hemoglobin, AST, ALT, bilirubin, creatinine, BUN, alkaline_phosphatase, NLR, CRP) add no independent signal.

analyses.code: smf.ols of full vs reduced model; F-test for joint significance of the secondary lab block
analyses.result_summary: In the joint adjusted model, all of {sodium, potassium, calcium, hemoglobin, AST, ALT, bilirubin, creatinine, BUN, alkaline_phosphatase, NLR, CRP} have p > 0.10 (most p > 0.4) and coefficients < 0.005 in absolute value on the natural PFS scale. Joint F-test for these 12 features p > 0.20 → no independent prognostic signal; all systematic variation in PFS is captured by ECOG, stage, brain mets, histology, smoking, age, albumin, weight loss, LDH, sex, KRAS G12C, ALK fusion, BRCA2, and sotorasib treatment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Refined: The maximum-effect treatment subgroup in the dataset is patients with kras_g12c=1, sex_female=0, alk_fusion=0, and brca2_mutation=0, where treatment_sotorasib raises pfs_months by ≈ +5 months.
Within the kras_g12c=1, sex_female=0 subgroup, neither alk_fusion=1 nor brca2_mutation=1 alone leaves any residual sotorasib benefit (both fully abolish the effect).

analyses.result_summary: n=3266 subset. Sotorasib on: mean PFS 8.234 (n=1154); off: 3.249 (n=2112); diff +4.985, p ≈ 0. Confirms the largest subgroup treatment effect.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind in (kras_g12c==1)&(sex_female==0)&(alk_fusion==1) and same with brca2_mutation==1
analyses.result_summary: ALK+ G12C+ male (n=138): diff -0.285, p=0.51. BRCA2+ G12C+ male (n=107): diff -0.068, p=0.88. Either suppressor abolishes the benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There are NO additional treatment-effect heterogeneities of comparable magnitude (>1 mo PFS) for any of the four treatments hidden in the data; the sotorasib-in-G12C+-males-without-ALK-or-BRCA2 effect is unique.

analyses.code: Maximum within-subgroup treatment effect across all 2-feature combinations × 4 treatments × 2x2 strata (~1500 candidate subgroups)
analyses.result_summary: Only sotorasib produces any subgroup with within-group PFS gain > 1 month at any p<0.05. The maximum non-sotorasib subgroup effect is < 0.25 mo. Confirms uniqueness.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After conditioning on the sotorasib×kras_g12c×sex three-way interaction, KRAS G12C is associated with WORSE baseline PFS (negative main effect on pfs_months in non-sotorasib-treated patients).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ kras_g12c + treatment_sotorasib + kras_g12c:treatment_sotorasib + ecog_ps + stage_iv + has_brain_mets', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted main effect of kras_g12c (in untreated patients) = -0.141 mo, p = 6e-6. KRAS G12C is itself a negative prognostic marker; it appears positive marginally only because sotorasib (randomized) gives huge benefit to half of G12C+ patients (males without ALK/BRCA2 suppressors).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative TMB-high effect on pfs_months survives multivariable adjustment for ECOG, stage, brain mets, histology, smoking, age, sex, and biomarkers.
Albumin × ECOG interaction on pfs_months is non-zero (high albumin partially rescues poor ECOG patients).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS coefficient on tmb_high in joint model
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted tmb_high coefficient = -0.007, p = 0.40. The univariate negative TMB effect (diff=-0.190) is fully confounded by other features; adjusted effect is null.

Interaction coefficient ≈ +0.12 per (g/dL × ECOG unit), p ≈ 0.001. Suggests albumin matters more in higher-ECOG patients but the effect is small and not central to the main treatment-heterogeneity story.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Females in this cohort have lower pfs_months than males primarily because females cannot benefit from sotorasib in KRAS G12C+ disease (i.e. the sex effect on PFS is concentrated in the kras_g12c=1, treatment_sotorasib=1 subgroup).

analyses.code: Stratified mean PFS by sex_female within (kras_g12c==1)&(treatment_sotorasib==1) and within complement
analyses.result_summary: Within sotorasib-treated G12C+ patients: male mean 7.857 vs female mean 3.211, gap = 4.65 mo. In G12C+ untreated: 3.213 vs 3.223, gap ≈ 0. In G12C-: gap ≈ 0.08 mo across treatment. Confirms the female PFS deficit is overwhelmingly attributable to the absence of sotorasib response in G12C+ females.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL best-supported treatment-effect subgroup hypotheses for each treatment on pfs_months: (a) treatment_sotorasib confers a +5 mo PFS gain in patients with kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=0 AND alk_fusion=0 AND brca2_mutation=0; (b) treatment_pembrolizumab provides no PFS benefit in any identifiable subgroup; (c) treatment_olaparib provides no PFS benefit in any identifiable subgroup, including BRCA2-mutant patients; (d) treatment_osimertinib provides no PFS benefit in any identifiable subgroup, including EGFR-mutant patients.

analyses.code: Final stratified comparisons summarized for each treatment
analyses.result_summary: (a) Sotorasib in KRAS G12C+ males without ALK fusion or BRCA2 mutation: +4.985 mo PFS (n=3266, p≈0). Subgroup definition is fully specified by kras_g12c=1, sex_female=0, alk_fusion=0, brca2_mutation=0. (b) Pembrolizumab: no positive subgroup; minor negative effects in stage_iv=0 (-0.10 mo) and low-weight-loss patients (-0.11 mo) – likely noise/multiple-testing. (c) Olaparib: no positive subgroup; BRCA2-mutant subgroup yields diff -0.022, p=0.85. (d) Osimertinib: no positive subgroup; EGFR-mutant subgroup yields diff -0.027, p=0.74. Sotorasib-in-G12C+-males is the only clinically meaningful targeted-treatment effect in this cohort.
